keyPoints: Reiterate central research question and causal pathway from diversity prompt to perceived conversational diversity and social capital (4–8 week field trial).
Ground design in online social capital theory (bonding/bridging/linking) and multi-dimensional measurement (cognitive, relational, structural).
Distinguish primary outcomes (perceived diversity, belonging) from secondary social-capital proxies (network measures, engagement).
Ensure a rigorous longitudinal field design (randomized or cluster-randomized) with baseline, mid-point, endline, and optional follow-up.
Specify the diversity-prompt implementation (three distinct perspectives) and logging of per-reply perspectives, length, source attribution, and engagement.
Prioritize ethics, governance, transparency, opt-out, and safety safeguards per NIST SP 1270 guidance.
Predefine analyses: mixed-effects models, longitudinal network analysis, mediation/moderation, and preregistration for transparency.
Anticipate heterogeneity and boundary conditions (user type, platform context, identity compatibility) and discuss diffusion dynamics and potential harms.
